sentences.zh: 反过来，该公司签署了一项协议，通过主要的 CoreWeave 实体向 OpenAI 提供计算。

sentences.en: The deal also features a “cash trap” that means that if either CoreWeave screws up (IE: doesn’t deliver the compute) and OpenAI can quit or OpenAI doesn’t pay for three months, the SPV stops feeding any money to CoreWeave until the situation is cured, and if OpenAI (or someone else) doesn’t start paying, things start to break, as the deal has a contract realization ratio of .85x,.
sentences.zh: 该交易还存在一个“现金陷阱”，这意味着如果 CoreWeave 搞砸了（即：不提供计算）而 OpenAI 可以退出，或者 OpenAI 在三个月内不付款，SPV 将停止向 CoreWeave 提供任何资金，直到情况得到解决，如果 OpenAI（或其他人）不开始付款，事情就会开始破裂，因为该交易的合同实现率为 0.85 倍。

blocks.html_en: To be clear, “non-recourse” does not mean “CoreWeave gets off scot free if these SPVs collapse,” just that creditors can jump on the SPV’s assets first and cannot immediately go after CoreWeave’s assets, though because each of these deals is guaranteed by the parent company (CoreWeave itself), it will eventually be forced to make them whole.

sentences.en: To be clear, “non-recourse” does not mean “CoreWeave gets off scot free if these SPVs collapse,” just that creditors can jump on the SPV’s assets first and cannot immediately go after CoreWeave’s assets, though because each of these deals is guaranteed by the parent company (CoreWeave itself), it will eventually be forced to make them whole.
sentences.zh: 需要明确的是，“无追索权”并不意味着“如果这些 SPV 倒闭，CoreWeave 就可以逍遥法外”，只是债权人可以先抢占 SPV 的资产，而不能立即追索 CoreWeave 的资产，但由于每笔交易都有母公司（CoreWeave 本身）担保，最终将被迫将它们整合在一起。

blocks.html_en: How Hyperscalers Hide Data Center Spending Obligations From Investors

sentences.en: How Hyperscalers Hide Data Center Spending Obligations From Investors
sentences.zh: 超大规模企业如何向投资者隐瞒数据中心支出义务

blocks.html_en: The nature of these SPVs makes it difficult to quantify the exact scale of data center debt, but Bloomberg estimates that there’s over $500 billion in outstanding AI data center debt, with (per Garima Kapoor of Elara Securities Research) at least $200 billion of it held by private credit, making up roughly 8% of outstanding private credit loans.

sentences.en: The nature of these SPVs makes it difficult to quantify the exact scale of data center debt, but Bloomberg estimates that there’s over $500 billion in outstanding AI data center debt, with (per Garima Kapoor of Elara Securities Research) at least $200 billion of it held by private credit, making up roughly 8% of outstanding private credit loans.
sentences.zh: 这些 SPV 的性质使得很难量化数据中心债务的确切规模，但彭博社估计，人工智能数据中心的未偿债务超过 5000 亿美元，其中（根据 Elara 证券研究公司的 Garima Kapoor 的数据）至少有 2000 亿美元由私人信贷持有，约占未偿私人信贷贷款的 8%。

blocks.html_en: That being said, the number is likely much higher. Nikkei Asia reported this week that Meta, Google, Amazon, Microsoft and Oracle have accrued around $1.65 trillion in outstanding debt in the last five years, with an additional hundreds of billions of dollars’ worth of “off balance sheet” debt, meaning that the corporate structure allows the company to not include it as part of its liabilities.

sentences.en: That being said, the number is likely much higher.
sentences.zh: 话虽如此，这个数字可能要高得多。

sentences.en: Nikkei Asia reported this week that Meta, Google, Amazon, Microsoft and Oracle have accrued around $1.65 trillion in outstanding debt in the last five years, with an additional hundreds of billions of dollars’ worth of “off balance sheet” debt, meaning that the corporate structure allows the company to not include it as part of its liabilities.
sentences.zh: 《日经亚洲》本周报道称，Meta、谷歌、亚马逊、微软和甲骨文在过去五年中累积了约 1.65 万亿美元的未偿债务，还有价值数千亿美元的“表外”债务，这意味着公司结构允许该公司不将其纳入其负债的一部分。

blocks.html_en: For example, BlackRock is currently raising $12 billion to build a data center for Meta, which in practical terms means BlackRock has invested in and is raising debt for a holding company called “Project Sopaipilla Holdings,” of which it owns 80% and Meta owns 20%. This holding company will then buy NVIDIA GPUs and pay construction firms to build the data center, and Meta’s (theoretical) payments will be used to pay down the debt.

sentences.en: For example, BlackRock is currently raising $12 billion to build a data center for Meta, which in practical terms means BlackRock has invested in and is raising debt for a holding company called “Project Sopaipilla Holdings,” of which it owns 80% and Meta owns 20%.
sentences.zh: 例如，贝莱德目前正在筹集 120 亿美元为 Meta 建设数据中心，这实际上意味着贝莱德投资了一家名为“Project Sopaipilla Holdings”的控股公司并为其筹集债务，贝莱德拥有该公司 80% 的股份，Meta 拥有 20% 的股份。

sentences.en: This holding company will then buy NVIDIA GPUs and pay construction firms to build the data center, and Meta’s (theoretical) payments will be used to pay down the debt.
sentences.zh: 然后，该控股公司将购买 NVIDIA GPU 并向建筑公司支付建造数据中心的费用，而 Meta 的（理论）付款将用于偿还债务。

blocks.html_en: Despite the fact that Meta will (theoretically) own and operate as the exclusive tenant of this data center, the actual debt — $12 billion or more! — won’t appear on its balance sheet, much like its $27 billion Hyperion Data Center that belongs to an SPV called Beignet Investor LLC which is 80% owned by Blue Owl, 20% owned by Meta, and funded using bond sales to PIMCO and BlackRock.

sentences.en: Despite the fact that Meta will (theoretically) own and operate as the exclusive tenant of this data center, the actual debt — $12 billion or more! — won’t appear on its balance sheet, much like its $27 billion Hyperion Data Center that belongs to an SPV called Beignet Investor LLC which is 80% owned by Blue Owl, 20% owned by Meta, and funded using bond sales to PIMCO and BlackRock.
sentences.zh: 尽管 Meta（理论上）将作为该数据中心的独家租户拥有并运营，但实际债务 — 120 亿美元或更多！ - 不会出现在其资产负债表上，就像价值 270 亿美元的 Hyperion 数据中心一样，该数据中心属于一家名为 Beignet Investor LLC 的 SPV，该公司由 Blue Owl 持有 80% 股份，Meta 持有 20% 股份，并通过向 PIMCO 和 BlackRock 出售债券筹集资金。

blocks.html_en: The problem with these SPV-based deals is that they allow companies to, at least on a balance sheet basis, hide the scale of their debts. Meta’s long term debt sits, as of its latest quarter, at around $58.7 billion. It’s as if the $39 billion in debt for gigawatts’ worth of AI data centers doesn’t exist out of the payments it’ll eventually have to make.

sentences.en: The problem with these SPV-based deals is that they allow companies to, at least on a balance sheet basis, hide the scale of their debts.
sentences.zh: 这些基于 SPV 的交易的问题在于，它们允许公司至少在资产负债表上隐藏其债务规模。

sentences.en: Meta’s long term debt sits, as of its latest quarter, at around $58.7 billion.
sentences.zh: 截至最近一个季度，Meta 的长期债务约为 587 亿美元。

sentences.en: It’s as if the $39 billion in debt for gigawatts’ worth of AI data centers doesn’t exist out of the payments it’ll eventually have to make.
sentences.zh: 就好像价值 100 亿瓦的人工智能数据中心的 390 亿美元债务在它最终必须支付的费用中并不存在。

blocks.html_en: Per Amanda Iacone of Bloomberg:

sentences.en: Per Amanda Iacone of Bloomberg:
sentences.zh: 彭博社的阿曼达·亚康 (Amanda Iacone) 表示：

blocks.html_en:
Enron Corp. exploited US accounting rules to hide from investors and lenders hundreds of millions in debt it had bundled into off-balance sheet entities — obligations that contributed to one of the biggest corporate collapses in US history.

Alphabet Inc. and Meta Platforms Inc. each have turned to vehicles known as variable interest entities (VIEs) as part of the financing mix needed to construct data centers and related energy infrastructure.

Meta, the parent of Facebook, last year formed a joint venture, a VIE, to build a Louisiana data center through a partnership with Blue Owl Capital. The social media titan’s maximum exposure for the venture is $46 billion, according to its filings with the Securities and Exchange Commission. The company announced last week that it would expand its planned campus and is expected to spend as much as $250 billion on the project, Bloomberg News has reported.

blocks.html_en: Meta determined it shouldn’t bring billions in debt from the Louisiana project onto its own balance sheet because it isn’t responsible for finding tenants to replace or join it at the nearly 4,000-acre campus — a critical job that impacts the entity’s economic performance, the social media company said in its most recent quarterly SEC filing. Meta said its role is limited to construction management, along with administrative and property management services.

sentences.en: Meta determined it shouldn’t bring billions in debt from the Louisiana project onto its own balance sheet because it isn’t responsible for finding tenants to replace or join it at the nearly 4,000-acre campus — a critical job that impacts the entity’s economic performance, the social media company said in its most recent quarterly SEC filing.
sentences.zh: Meta 决定不应该将路易斯安那项目的数十亿美元债务纳入自己的资产负债表，因为它不负责寻找租户来取代或加入这个占地近 4,000 英亩的园区——这是一项影响该实体经济表现的关键工作，这家社交媒体公司在其最近向 SEC 提交的季度文件中表示。

sentences.en: Meta said its role is limited to construction management, along with administrative and property management services.
sentences.zh: Meta 表示，其职责仅限于建筑管理以及行政和物业管理服务。

blocks.html_en: Auditor Ernst & Young raised a “red flag” (per the WSJ) about this arrangement, flagging it as a “critical audit matter,” adding that it “...was especially challenging due to the significant judgment required in determining the activities that most significantly affect the VIE’s economic performance.” Nevertheless, it was approved, it happened, and everything is fine and normal.

sentences.en: Auditor Ernst & Young raised a “red flag” (per the WSJ) about this arrangement, flagging it as a “critical audit matter,” adding that it “...was especially challenging due to the significant judgment required in determining the activities that most significantly affect the VIE’s economic performance.”
sentences.zh: 审计师安永会计师事务所（根据《华尔街日报》）对这一安排提出了“危险信号”，将其标记为“关键审计事项”，并补充说，“……特别具有挑战性，因为在确定对 VIE 经济绩效影响最大的活动时需要做出重大判断。”

sentences.en: Nevertheless, it was approved, it happened, and everything is fine and normal.
sentences.zh: 然而，它被批准了，它发生了，一切都很好，很正常。

blocks.html_en: This is why Google backstopped Fluidstack and Cipher Mining’s 300MW data center and another for TeraWulf. Both will, eventually, operate as data centers that Google will lease to provide compute to Anthropic, booking revenue for doing so, acting as the sole tenant and the entire reason that the debt was raised, yet because Fluidstack and TeraWulf and Cipher Mining are the actual entities involved, nothing shows up on Google’s balance sheet.

sentences.en: This is why Google backstopped Fluidstack and Cipher Mining’s 300MW data center and another for TeraWulf.
sentences.zh: 这就是 Google 支持 Fluidstack 和 Cipher Mining 的 300MW 数据中心以及 TeraWulf 的另一个数据中心的原因。

sentences.en: Both will, eventually, operate as data centers that Google will lease to provide compute to Anthropic, booking revenue for doing so, acting as the sole tenant and the entire reason that the debt was raised, yet because Fluidstack and TeraWulf and Cipher Mining are the actual entities involved, nothing shows up on Google’s balance sheet.
sentences.zh: 最终，这两个数据中心都将作为数据中心运营，谷歌将租赁这些数据中心为 Anthropic 提供计算服务，并作为唯一租户和筹集债务的全部原因记录这样做的收入，但由于 Fluidstack、TeraWulf 和 Cipher Mining 是实际涉及的实体，因此谷歌的资产负债表上没有显示任何内容。

blocks.html_en: What’s also important to note is that none of the money going into these SPVs counts as capital expenditures. For example, across the space of five quarters (Q1 2025 through Q1 2026), Meta spent around $88.6 billion in capital expenditures, but that doesn’t include any of the debt or purchases of GPUs or anything else done in its name as part of the Hyperion SPV, despite it having (per its own fillings) $45.95 billion of exposure.

sentences.en: What’s also important to note is that none of the money going into these SPVs counts as capital expenditures.
sentences.zh: 还需要注意的是，进入这些 SPV 的资金都不算资本支出。

sentences.en: For example, across the space of five quarters (Q1 2025 through Q1 2026), Meta spent around $88.6 billion in capital expenditures, but that doesn’t include any of the debt or purchases of GPUs or anything else done in its name as part of the Hyperion SPV, despite it having (per its own fillings) $45.95 billion of exposure.
sentences.zh: 例如，在五个季度（2025 年第一季度到 2026 年第一季度）的时间里，Meta 花费了约 886 亿美元的资本支出，但这不包括任何债务或购买 GPU 或作为 Hyperion SPV 的一部分以其名义所做的任何其他事情，尽管它（根据其自己的填充）拥有 459.5 亿美元的风险敞口。

blocks.html_en: AI Data Centers Are The AI Bubble’s Subprime Mortgages

sentences.en: AI Data Centers Are The AI Bubble’s Subprime Mortgages
sentences.zh: 人工智能数据中心是人工智能泡沫的次级抵押贷款

blocks.html_en: I want to be very blunt about something: we do not, at this point, have a firm hand on exactly how much demand there is for AI compute, and evidence suggests that it’s much, much smaller than we’ve been led to believe.

sentences.en: I want to be very blunt about something: we do not, at this point, have a firm hand on exactly how much demand there is for AI compute, and evidence suggests that it’s much, much smaller than we’ve been led to believe.
sentences.zh: 我想直言不讳地说：目前，我们还无法准确掌握人工智能计算的需求量，而且有证据表明，这一需求比我们想象的要小得多。

blocks.html_en: I estimate that 70% or more of Microsoft, Google and Amazon’s compute capacity is taken up by OpenAI and Anthropic, and in my analysis of non-hyperscale compute providers, I struggled to find any customer other than them that was spending more than $50 million a year on compute.

sentences.en: I estimate that 70% or more of Microsoft, Google and Amazon’s compute capacity is taken up by OpenAI and Anthropic, and in my analysis of non-hyperscale compute providers, I struggled to find any customer other than them that was spending more than $50 million a year on compute.
sentences.zh: 我估计，微软、谷歌和亚马逊 70% 或更多的计算能力被 OpenAI 和 Anthropic 占用，在我对非超大规模计算提供商的分析中，我很难找到除了他们之外的任何客户，每年在计算上的支出超过 5000 万美元。

blocks.html_en: That’s because real, diverse demand does not exist for AI compute, as evidenced by the fact that the same four or five companies are the only ones interested in renting it at scale.

sentences.en: That’s because real, diverse demand does not exist for AI compute, as evidenced by the fact that the same four or five companies are the only ones interested in renting it at scale.
sentences.zh: 这是因为人工智能计算并不存在真正的、多样化的需求，事实证明，只有四五家公司有兴趣大规模租赁人工智能计算。

blocks.html_en: Sidenote: Furthermore, any demand for compute that exists currently is inflated by the effective subsidization of paying subscribers to ChatGPT and Claude, who can burn tens of thousands of dollars worth of tokens while only paying $200-a-month.

sentences.en: Sidenote: Furthermore, any demand for compute that exists currently is inflated by the effective subsidization of paying subscribers to ChatGPT and Claude, who can burn tens of thousands of dollars worth of tokens while only paying $200-a-month.
sentences.zh: 旁注：此外，当前存在的任何计算需求都会因 ChatGPT 和 Claude 付费订户的有效补贴而膨胀，他们每月只需支付 200 美元，就可以燃烧价值数万美元的代币。

blocks.html_en: For example, on July 1, Bloomberg reported that Meta (mere months after Zuckerberg said that “selling capacity was on the table if it overbuilt”) was creating a cloud business to rent out its AI GPU capacity. A mere two weeks later, the New York Times reported that it was in talks to rent capacity to Anthropic.

sentences.en: For example, on July 1, Bloomberg reported that Meta (mere months after Zuckerberg said that “selling capacity was on the table if it overbuilt”) was creating a cloud business to rent out its AI GPU capacity.
sentences.zh: 例如，彭博社 7 月 1 日报道称，Meta（就在扎克伯格表示“如果过度建设，则可以出售产能”几个月后）正在创建一项云业务来出租其 AI GPU 产能。

sentences.en: A mere two weeks later, the New York Times reported that it was in talks to rent capacity to Anthropic.
sentences.zh: 仅仅两周后，《纽约时报》报道称，该公司正在就向 Anthropic 租赁产能进行谈判。

blocks.html_en: While one might argue that Meta is taking advantage of a wealthy buyer, one has to ask: if there was such insatiable demand for compute, why wouldn’t it want to sell it to a diverse set of customers who would likely pay a much higher rate than a years-long contract?

sentences.en: While one might argue that Meta is taking advantage of a wealthy buyer, one has to ask: if there was such insatiable demand for compute, why wouldn’t it want to sell it to a diverse set of customers who would likely pay a much higher rate than a years-long contract?
sentences.zh: 虽然有人可能会认为 Meta 正在利用富有的买家，但人们不得不问：如果对计算的需求如此难以满足，为什么它不想将其出售给不同的客户群，而这些客户可能会支付比一年期合同高得多的价格？

blocks.html_en: It’s because those customers do not exist at a scale that would actually make it worthwhile! If they did, we’d see massive bursts of remaining performance obligations from neoclouds like Nebius, IREN and CoreWeave that were unrelated to new contracts they’ve signed with either hyperscalers, OpenAI or Anthropic. Companies like Lightning, Runpod, and Lambda would have billions in revenue. Instead, Runpod has $120 million in ARR, $500 million in ‘annualized’ revenue, and Lambda had $114 million in revenue as of the second quarter of 2025, with a little less than half of that coming from Microsoft and Amazon.

sentences.en: It’s because those customers do not exist at a scale that would actually make it worthwhile!
sentences.zh: 这是因为这些客户的存在规模并不值得！

sentences.en: If they did, we’d see massive bursts of remaining performance obligations from neoclouds like Nebius, IREN and CoreWeave that were unrelated to new contracts they’ve signed with either hyperscalers, OpenAI or Anthropic.
sentences.zh: 如果他们这样做了，我们会看到 Nebius、IREN 和 CoreWeave 等 Neocloud 的剩余履约义务大量激增，这些义务与他们与超大规模企业、OpenAI 或 Anthropic 签署的新合同无关。

sentences.en: Companies like Lightning, Runpod, and Lambda would have billions in revenue.
sentences.zh: 像 Lightning、Runpod 和 Lambda 这样的公司将拥有数十亿美元的收入。

sentences.en: Instead, Runpod has $120 million in ARR, $500 million in ‘annualized’ revenue, and Lambda had $114 million in revenue as of the second quarter of 2025, with a little less than half of that coming from Microsoft and Amazon.
sentences.zh: 相反，截至 2025 年第二季度，Runpod 的 ARR 为 1.2 亿美元，“年化”收入为 5 亿美元，Lambda 的收入为 1.14 亿美元，其中不到一半来自微软和亚马逊。

blocks.html_en: While the counter-argument is that these companies are all GPU-constrained, and that demand is simply waiting in the wings…except surely that would mean that these companies also had massive remaining performance obligations?

sentences.en: While the counter-argument is that these companies are all GPU-constrained, and that demand is simply waiting in the wings…except surely that would mean that these companies also had massive remaining performance obligations?
sentences.zh: 虽然反驳意见是这些公司都受到 GPU 的限制，而且这种需求只是在等待……但这肯定意味着这些公司还有大量的剩余性能义务吗？

blocks.html_en: To be clear, the point I’m making is not that there’s no demand, just that the vast majority of that demand is coming from either Anthropic and OpenAI — two companies that cannot afford to pay for it long-term — and hyperscalers, who are mostly buying compute on behalf of OpenAI and Anthropic.

sentences.en: To be clear, the point I’m making is not that there’s no demand, just that the vast majority of that demand is coming from either Anthropic and OpenAI — two companies that cannot afford to pay for it long-term — and hyperscalers, who are mostly buying compute on behalf of OpenAI and Anthropic.
sentences.zh: 需要明确的是，我要表达的观点并不是没有需求，而是绝大多数需求来自 Anthropic 和 OpenAI（两家无法长期支付费用的公司）以及超大规模企业，这些企业主要代表 OpenAI 和 Anthropic 购买计算。

blocks.html_en: And I’m not sure that people are taking me seriously when I say that AI compute demand does not exist at the scale that it needs to, will likely never reach that scale, and data center construction is a debt-funded asset bubble with ruinous consequences.

sentences.en: And I’m not sure that people are taking me seriously when I say that AI compute demand does not exist at the scale that it needs to, will likely never reach that scale, and data center construction is a debt-funded asset bubble with ruinous consequences.
sentences.zh: 当我说人工智能计算需求并未达到所需的规模，并且很可能永远不会达到那样的规模，而数据中心建设是一个债务融资的资产泡沫，会带来毁灭性的后果时，我不确定人们是否认真对待我。

sentences.en: How The Subprime Data Center Crisis Manifests
sentences.zh: 次贷数据中心危机如何显现

blocks.html_en: So, let’s set some table stakes.

sentences.en: So, let’s set some table stakes.
sentences.zh: 那么，让我们设定一些赌注。

blocks.html_en: Per my own analysis, NVIDIA’s predicted $1 trillion in Blackwell and Vera Rubin GPU sales (by the end of 2027) represents around 40GW of data center capacity, which will, assuming a PUE of 1.35, result in around 30GW of usable capacity. At a cost of around $12 million a megawatt, that works out to around $435 billion in global annual compute revenue to make these data centers necessary.

sentences.en: Per my own analysis, NVIDIA’s predicted $1 trillion in Blackwell and Vera Rubin GPU sales (by the end of 2027) represents around 40GW of data center capacity, which will, assuming a PUE of 1.35, result in around 30GW of usable capacity.
sentences.zh: 根据我自己的分析，NVIDIA 预计 Blackwell 和 Vera Rubin GPU 销售额将达到 1 万亿美元（到 2027 年底），这意味着数据中心容量约为 40GW，假设 PUE 为 1.35，则可用容量约为 30GW。

sentences.en: At a cost of around $12 million a megawatt, that works out to around $435 billion in global annual compute revenue to make these data centers necessary.
sentences.zh: 以每兆瓦约 1200 万美元的成本计算，全球每年的计算收入约为 4350 亿美元，因此这些数据中心必不可少。

blocks.html_en: Right now, there appears to be roughly $100 billion or so in annual compute spend, with OpenAI representing around $50 billion (per their statements in the Musk trial), and Anthropic likely spending similar amounts. Microsoft and NVIDIA represent a combined 65% of CoreWeave’s $2.08 billion in (latest) quarterly revenue, with the rest likely taken up by OpenAI. IREN, another neocloud, recently announced it was targeting a year-end cloud ARR of “over $4 billion,” or around $333 million a month, with a customer base that includes, unsurprisingly, Microsoft and NVIDIA, as well as companies like Perplexity, Figure AI, and Together AI that are unlikely to be spending more than $50 million apiece given their funding status and revenues.

sentences.en: Right now, there appears to be roughly $100 billion or so in annual compute spend, with OpenAI representing around $50 billion (per their statements in the Musk trial), and Anthropic likely spending similar amounts.
sentences.zh: 目前，每年的计算支出似乎约为 1000 亿美元左右，其中 OpenAI 约占 500 亿美元（根据他们在 Musk 审判中的声明），Anthropic 可能会花费类似的金额。

sentences.en: Microsoft and NVIDIA represent a combined 65% of CoreWeave’s $2.08 billion in (latest) quarterly revenue, with the rest likely taken up by OpenAI.
sentences.zh: 微软和 NVIDIA 合计占 CoreWeave 20.8 亿美元（最新）季度收入的 65%，其余部分可能由 OpenAI 占据。

sentences.en: IREN, another neocloud, recently announced it was targeting a year-end cloud ARR of “over $4 billion,” or around $333 million a month, with a customer base that includes, unsurprisingly, Microsoft and NVIDIA, as well as companies like Perplexity, Figure AI, and Together AI that are unlikely to be spending more than $50 million apiece given their funding status and revenues.
sentences.zh: 另一家 Neocloud IREN 最近宣布，其年终云 ARR 目标为“超过 40 亿美元”，即每月约 3.33 亿美元，不出所料，其客户群包括微软和 NVIDIA，以及 Perplexity、Figure AI 和 Together AI 等公司，考虑到它们的融资状况和收入，这些公司的支出不太可能超过 5000 万美元。

blocks.html_en: Sidenote: It’s important to note that spend isn’t necessarily the same as utilization. Much of the compute spend comes in the form of long-term contracts, where the price-per-hour for a GPU is  (naturally) lower than it would be if it was rented to deal with a momentary spike in utilization. The vast majority of AI compute spend comes in the form of these long-term contracts.

sentences.en: Sidenote: It’s important to note that spend isn’t necessarily the same as utilization.
sentences.zh: 旁注：需要注意的是，支出不一定与利用率相同。

sentences.en: Much of the compute spend comes in the form of long-term contracts, where the price-per-hour for a GPU is (naturally) lower than it would be if it was rented to deal with a momentary spike in utilization.
sentences.zh: 大部分计算支出以长期合同的形式出现，其中 GPU 的每小时价格（自然）低于租用 GPU 来应对瞬时利用率峰值的价格。

sentences.en: The vast majority of AI compute spend comes in the form of these long-term contracts.
sentences.zh: 绝大多数人工智能计算支出都是以这些长期合同的形式出现的。

blocks.html_en: Another concerning anti-demand signal is the fact that NVIDIA has committed to $30 billion in multi-year cloud compute agreements, spending $6 billion or more a year through 2028 to rent back its GPUs, including a $6.3 billion backstop for CoreWeave that explicitly states that NVIDIA is “is obligated to purchase the residual unsold capacity” through April 2032, suggesting that there would be residual capacity that had gone unsold to the tune of billions of dollars.

sentences.en: Another concerning anti-demand signal is the fact that NVIDIA has committed to $30 billion in multi-year cloud compute agreements, spending $6 billion or more a year through 2028 to rent back its GPUs, including a $6.3 billion backstop for CoreWeave that explicitly states that NVIDIA is “is obligated to purchase the residual unsold capacity” through April 2032, suggesting that there would be residual capacity that had gone unsold to the tune of billions of dollars.
sentences.zh: 另一个令人担忧的反需求信号是，NVIDIA 已承诺签署 300 亿美元的多年云计算协议，到 2028 年每年花费 60 亿美元或更多来租回 GPU，其中包括为 CoreWeave 提供 63 亿美元的支持，明确规定 NVIDIA “有义务购买截至 2032 年 4 月的剩余未售产能”，这表明未售出的剩余产能将达到数十亿美元。

blocks.html_en: Oh, and NVIDIA owns 9.3% of Nebius too. It’s also invested in IREN, CoreWeave and has both invested in and rented capacity from Lambda.

sentences.en: Oh, and NVIDIA owns 9.3% of Nebius too.
sentences.zh: 哦，NVIDIA 还拥有 Nebius 9.3% 的股份。

sentences.en: It’s also invested in IREN, CoreWeave and has both invested in and rented capacity from Lambda.
sentences.zh: 它还投资了 IREN、CoreWeave，并投资和租用了 Lambda 的容量。

blocks.html_en: If you’re wondering why these deals keep getting signed — as mentioned previously — it’s because a financial guarantee from NVIDIA is sufficient collateral for a bank to lend money to these companies to buy more GPUs.

sentences.en: If you’re wondering why these deals keep getting signed — as mentioned previously — it’s because a financial guarantee from NVIDIA is sufficient collateral for a bank to lend money to these companies to buy more GPUs.
sentences.zh: 如果您想知道为什么这些交易不断签署（如前所述），这是因为 NVIDIA 的财务担保足以让银行向这些公司贷款以购买更多 GPU。

blocks.html_en: I imagine a conversation in the Big Short 2 might go a little like this scene.

sentences.en: I imagine a conversation in the Big Short 2 might go a little like this scene.
sentences.zh: 我想《大空头2》中的对话可能会有点像这个场景。

blocks.html_en:
HUANG: So there’re these companies I invest in that, at least in theory, build data centers using my AI GPUs, but I need them to buy more GPUs, so I sign a contract saying that I’ll rent the GPUs back from them in the future. Because NVIDIA has such a strong balance sheet, these companies can raise billions of dollars to buy my GPUs just because I promised to rent them in the future, and the best part is all the risk is held by the companies and the investors. When I need more money, I just sign another contract, they raise more debt, I sell more GPUs. 

BAUM: So — just so I have this clearly — you, the guy who makes the GPUs, invest in companies that exist pretty much to buy GPUs from you and rent them to customers. Except you’re the customer too, and a big one.

HUANG: That’s right. We call them neoclouds.

S&P just revised CoreWeave’s outlook to positive.

BAUM: That’s fucking crazy.

HUANG: It’s not crazy — it’s awesome.

blocks.html_en: Those Meta and Microsoft neocloud deals exist explicitly to lower their capex and debt — by which I mean that if Nebius or IREN takes on the billions in debt to buy all of those GPUs, Microsoft and Meta only have to worry about the ongoing leases, assuming that construction is ever complete. These deals also regularly include a clause that allows them to be terminated in the event that delivery milestones are not met, as is the case with Microsoft’s $17.4 billion deal with Nebius.

blocks.html_en: This means that hyperscalers take on effectively no risk, and investors are left holding the bag. For example, Nebius’ recent $775 million debt facility is “backed by contracted cashflows and deployed GPU infrastructure,” meaning that if things fall apart, the only entity that can be sued would be a company that explicitly exists to buy NVIDIA GPUs and rent them.

blocks.html_en: To be abundantly clear, the vast majority of the AI data center compute revenue is contingent on the continued ability of two unprofitable, unsustainable AI companies’ to raise tens or hundreds of billions of dollars a year. This is not an overstatement, this is not hyperbole, it is the quite literal situation we’re stuck in.

blocks.html_en: There Is Over 15 Times More Data Center Capacity Being Built Than Actual Data Center Demand, Which Is Already Inflated By OpenAI and Anthropic

sentences.en: There Is Over 15 Times More Data Center Capacity Being Built Than Actual Data Center Demand, Which Is Already Inflated By OpenAI and Anthropic
sentences.zh: 正在建设的数据中心容量比实际数据中心需求多 15 倍以上，而 OpenAI 和 Anthropic 已经夸大了实际数据中心需求

blocks.html_en: Putting aside whether data centers are profitable or not (they aren’t), if the demand does not exist at this remarkable scale, the vast majority of AI data centers and their associated SPVs will collapse.

sentences.en: Putting aside whether data centers are profitable or not (they aren’t), if the demand does not exist at this remarkable scale, the vast majority of AI data centers and their associated SPVs will collapse.
sentences.zh: 抛开数据中心是否有利可图（实际上没有），如果不存在如此显着规模的需求，绝大多数人工智能数据中心及其相关的 SPV 将崩溃。

blocks.html_en: If we take February’s Sightline Climate report at its word, there is 190GW of data center capacity in planning, or 140GW of IT load if we take a 1.35 PUE, for a total of $1.68 Trillion. If we assume — and I’m being nice! — that there’s $120 billion in annual compute demand, and take into account that tens of billions of dollars’ worth of data centers have been announced since, this means that there’s over 15 times more data centers being planned than the demand that actually exists, and 70% to 90% of that demand is from Anthropic and OpenAI’s unprofitable services.

sentences.zh: 如果我们按照 2 月份的 Sightline Climate 报告的说法，规划中的数据中心容量为 190GW，如果 PUE 为 1.35，则 IT 负载为 140GW，总计 1.68 万亿美元。

sentences.en: If we assume — and I’m being nice! — that there’s $120 billion in annual compute demand, and take into account that tens of billions of dollars’ worth of data centers have been announced since, this means that there’s over 15 times more data centers being planned than the demand that actually exists, and 70% to 90% of that demand is from Anthropic and OpenAI’s unprofitable services.
sentences.zh: 如果我们假设——而且我很好！ — 每年的计算需求为 1200 亿美元，并考虑到此后已宣布的价值数百亿美元的数据中心，这意味着规划中的数据中心数量比实际存在的需求多出 15 倍以上，而其中 70% 到 90% 的需求来自 Anthropic 和 OpenAI 不盈利的服务。

blocks.html_en: The Global Savings Grab, Or How Everybody Misrated The Risk Of AI Data Centers (An Misunderstood Demand)

sentences.en: The Global Savings Grab, Or How Everybody Misrated The Risk Of AI Data Centers (An Misunderstood Demand)
sentences.zh: 全球储蓄争夺，或者每个人如何错误评价人工智能数据中心的风险（被误解的需求）

blocks.html_en: The hunger for speculation has vastly outpaced the actual demand for AI compute, much like it did in the great financial crisis, and for many of the same reasons. Back in May, JP Morgan’s Karen Ward brought up the global savings glut that I mentioned in the intro as part of a discussion of what she calls a “global savings grab”:

sentences.zh: 对投机的渴望远远超过了对人工智能计算的实际需求，就像在金融危机中一样，并且出于许多相同的原因。

sentences.en: Back in May, JP Morgan’s Karen Ward brought up the global savings glut that I mentioned in the intro as part of a discussion of what she calls a “global savings grab”:
sentences.zh: 早在五月份，摩根大通的凯伦·沃德（Karen Ward）就我在介绍中提到的全球储蓄过剩问题进行了讨论，她称之为“全球储蓄抢夺”：

blocks.html_en:
In short, a number of Asian countries were either scarred from years of financial crisis and balance-of-payments turmoil in the 1990s, or seeking to depress their currencies to focus on export-driven growth. This created a glut of savings that travelled abroad, with US government bonds top of the wish list.

[US Fed Chair] Bernanke highlighted that this was acting as both a blessing and curse for the US. It provided the US Treasury secretary with abundant cheap financing and so too for American households and businesses. But this cheap capital was too tempting, running the risk of overspend and bubbles in the US. Within a few years, this warning proved correct.

blocks.html_en:
That wasn’t the end of the savings glut story, however. Stage two happened when key parts of Europe shifted from being net spenders to net savers after the Eurozone sovereign crisis.

The global savings glut got bigger, as did the investment flows into the US, one of the few areas of the world still happy to spend and grow. At the end of 2025, America had a negative net international investment position — the difference between US-owned foreign assets and foreign-owned US assets — of $27tn.

However, we are now transitioning to what could be termed a global savings grab. Governments and companies around the world want to spend more. In doing so, they are competing to offer the most attractive opportunities to the world’s savers.

blocks.html_en: The difference between a savings glut and savings grab is that there’s incredible demand for cash rather than an excess of capital to invest, at a time when banks (and private credit funds) have tons of cash but are pulling back from investing in software and healthcare companies due to AI-related risk… and investing in AI data centers, which they consider to be the “cheat code” — high-yield, low-risk investments in infrastructure that have “guaranteed” customers.

sentences.zh: 储蓄过剩和储蓄掠夺之间的区别在于，当银行（和私人信贷基金）拥有大量现金但由于人工智能相关风险而撤回对软件和医疗保健公司的投资时，对现金的需求令人难以置信，而不是投资资本过剩……

sentences.zh: 投资人工智能数据中心，他们认为这是“作弊码”——对“有保障”客户的基础设施进行高收益、低风险的投资。

blocks.html_en: It’s a perfect storm that mixes dangerously with the $400 billion or so in private infrastructure funds waiting to deploy, much of which is funded by pension and insurance funds (as I covered in the Hater’s Guide To Private Credit) drawn to private credit — get this! — because they needed new things to invest in after the Great Financial Crisis made yields difficult to find because banks were restricted from making the same kind of reckless bets that caused the global financial system to implode.

sentences.en: It’s a perfect storm that mixes dangerously with the $400 billion or so in private infrastructure funds waiting to deploy, much of which is funded by pension and insurance funds (as I covered in the Hater’s Guide To Private Credit) drawn to private credit — get this! — because they needed new things to invest in after the Great Financial Crisis made yields difficult to find because banks were restricted from making the same kind of reckless bets that caused the global financial system to implode.
sentences.zh: 这是一场完美的风暴，与等待部署的大约 4000 亿美元的私人基础设施基金危险地混合在一起，其中大部分资金是由吸引私人信贷的养老金和保险基金（正如我在《仇恨者的私人信贷指南》中介绍的那样）提供的——明白了！ ——因为在金融危机之后，他们需要新的东西来投资，因为银行受到限制，不能做出导致全球金融体系崩溃的同样鲁莽的押注，导致收益率难以获得。

How about the fact that the amount of dry powder within these retail-focused financial institutions is shrinking — suggesting that more and more cash is being deployed, or withdrawn as a result of diminishing confidence within households.

blocks.html_en: Anyway, much of the assumption of how “safe” investments in AI data centers comes down to three ideas:

sentences.zh: 无论如何，关于人工智能数据中心投资如何“安全”的大部分假设可以归结为三个想法：

blocks.html_en: • AI data center demand is infinite and all compute will be used.

blocks.html_en:
• AI data centers all have “locked-in customer demand.”
    • This is, to be clear, fundamentally untrue. The only guaranteed, locked-in customer demand I can find is from Amazon, Google and Microsoft (for OpenAI and Anthropic), Meta, and Google and Anthropic. While there might be some random AI firms or inference companies that have “locked up capacity,” their dollars are only as good as their access to venture capital, much like Anthropic and OpenAI.

sentences.en: The “proof” that it’s worth investing in data centers mostly comes down to seeing that hyperscalers are spending a lot of money on them, and that OpenAI and Anthropic have lots of demand for compute.
sentences.zh: 数据中心值得投资的“证据”主要归结为超大规模企业在数据中心上投入了大量资金，并且 OpenAI 和 Anthropic 对计算有大量需求。

blocks.html_en: They have also mistaken the ability for hyperscalers to keep funding data centers out of cashflow as a sign that all data centers are a good investment, when what’s actually happening is that they’ve run out of hypergrowth ideas and had so much free cash sloshing about that they were able to spend a trillion dollars in four years. Hyperscaler demand for NVIDIA chips has been so significant that it made it look like NVIDIA had an insane amount of demand, which in turn created a degree of FOMO and speculation, with everybody assuming that because hyperscalers were getting rich (they weren’t, they have never disclosed their AI revenues, but people just assume they wouldn’t do this without making a profit) that they too would get rich by buying GPUs and building data centers.

sentences.en: They have also mistaken the ability for hyperscalers to keep funding data centers out of cashflow as a sign that all data centers are a good investment, when what’s actually happening is that they’ve run out of hypergrowth ideas and had so much free cash sloshing about that they were able to spend a trillion dollars in four years.
sentences.zh: 他们还错误地认为，超大规模企业能够将数据中心的资金投入排除在现金流之外，这表明所有数据中心都是一项不错的投资，而实际情况是，他们已经耗尽了高速增长的想法，并且拥有大量的自由现金，以至于他们能够在四年内花费一万亿美元。

sentences.en: Hyperscaler demand for NVIDIA chips has been so significant that it made it look like NVIDIA had an insane amount of demand, which in turn created a degree of FOMO and speculation, with everybody assuming that because hyperscalers were getting rich (they weren’t, they have never disclosed their AI revenues, but people just assume they wouldn’t do this without making a profit) that they too would get rich by buying GPUs and building data centers.
sentences.zh: 超大规模企业对 NVIDIA 芯片的需求如此之大，以至于看起来 NVIDIA 的需求量非常大，这反过来又造成了一定程度的 FOMO 和投机，每个人都认为，由于超大规模企业正在致富（他们没有，他们从未披露过自己的 AI 收入，但人们只是假设他们不会在不盈利的情况下这样做），他们也会通过购买 GPU 和建设数据中心而致富。

blocks.html_en: NVIDIA has been a big part of creating this fake demand story with its investments in — and backstop contracts with — CoreWeave, Lambda, IREN, and Nebius. Much like people assume hyperscalers wouldn’t make a huge, trillion-dollar mistake, they also assume that NVIDIA wouldn’t invest in companies that weren’t going to see incredible demand, somehow ignoring the very obvious point that NVIDIA doesn’t give a shit about any neoclouds outside of their ability to generate more GPU sales.

sentences.en: NVIDIA has been a big part of creating this fake demand story with its investments in — and backstop contracts with — CoreWeave, Lambda, IREN, and Nebius.
sentences.zh: NVIDIA 通过对 CoreWeave、Lambda、IREN 和 Nebius 的投资以及与这些公司签订的支持合同，在制造这个虚假需求故事中发挥了重要作用。

sentences.en: Much like people assume hyperscalers wouldn’t make a huge, trillion-dollar mistake, they also assume that NVIDIA wouldn’t invest in companies that weren’t going to see incredible demand, somehow ignoring the very obvious point that NVIDIA doesn’t give a shit about any neoclouds outside of their ability to generate more GPU sales.
sentences.zh: 就像人们认为超大规模企业不会犯价值数万亿美元的巨大错误一样，他们也认为 NVIDIA 不会投资那些不会出现令人难以置信的需求的公司，不知何故忽略了一个非常明显的点，即除了产生更多 GPU 销量的能力之外，NVIDIA 并不关心任何新云。

blocks.html_en: This is where the media and analysts could’ve done their jobs, but because none of the neoclouds have done yet, it’s totally fine that CoreWeave is sat on $30 billion in debt, most of it impossible to pay if any client drops out of a contract, because, much like the great financial crisis, nothing bad had happened yet, by which I mean that clients leave their invoices unpaid, and CoreWeave finds itself in financial distress.

sentences.en: This is where the media and analysts could’ve done their jobs, but because none of the neoclouds have done yet, it’s totally fine that CoreWeave is sat on $30 billion in debt, most of it impossible to pay if any client drops out of a contract, because, much like the great financial crisis, nothing bad had happened yet, by which I mean that clients leave their invoices unpaid, and CoreWeave finds itself in financial distress.
sentences.zh: 这是媒体和分析师本可以完成他们的工作的地方，但因为新云还没有这样做，所以 CoreWeave 背负 300 亿美元的债务完全没问题，如果任何客户退出合同，大部分债务都无法支付，因为，就像伟大的金融危机一样，还没有发生任何糟糕的事情，我的意思是客户没有支付发票，而 CoreWeave 发现自己陷入了财务困境。

blocks.html_en: NVIDIA’s naked self-dealing and circular financing are only made possible with a completely captured tech and business media. While mildly-concerned stories have run for the last year or so about the “massive circular financing under the AI bubble,” none of them treat the situation as anything else other than a curiosity.

sentences.en: NVIDIA’s naked self-dealing and circular financing are only made possible with a completely captured tech and business media.
sentences.zh: 英伟达的赤裸裸的自我交易和循环融资只有在完全占领科技和商业媒体的情况下才有可能实现。

sentences.en: While mildly-concerned stories have run for the last year or so about the “massive circular financing under the AI bubble,” none of them treat the situation as anything else other than a curiosity.
sentences.zh: 尽管过去一年左右的时间里一直有关于“人工智能泡沫下的大规模循环融资”的温和担忧的故事，但没有一个人将这种情况视为除了好奇之外的其他任何事情。

blocks.html_en: I cannot adequately express my contempt for those that have hand-waved the danger of this bubble, or tried to minimize the risk created by the overbuild of AI data centers.

sentences.en: I cannot adequately express my contempt for those that have hand-waved the danger of this bubble, or tried to minimize the risk created by the overbuild of AI data centers.
sentences.zh: 我无法充分表达我对那些挥舞着泡沫危险，或试图将人工智能数据中心过度建设所带来的风险降至最低的人的蔑视。

blocks.html_en: The Systemic Risk of the Subprime Data Center Crisis

sentences.en: The Systemic Risk of the Subprime Data Center Crisis
sentences.zh: 次贷数据中心危机的系统性风险

blocks.html_en: Much like a subprime mortgage, AI data center debt is being poorly-underwritten, virtually-uncollateralized and issued to projects that have extremely low likelihoods of repayment, all based on flimsy information and hype-driven mania.

sentences.en: Much like a subprime mortgage, AI data center debt is being poorly-underwritten, virtually-uncollateralized and issued to projects that have extremely low likelihoods of repayment, all based on flimsy information and hype-driven mania.
sentences.zh: 就像次级抵押贷款一样，人工智能数据中心债务的承保情况不佳，几乎没有抵押，并且发行给偿还可能性极低的项目，所有这些都基于脆弱的信息和炒作驱动的狂热。

blocks.html_en: Their collapse is inevitable because their ongoing payments are made out of customer revenue that is, in the vast majority of cases, entirely theoretical or contingent on payments from unprofitable and unsustainable AI companies.

sentences.en: Their collapse is inevitable because their ongoing payments are made out of customer revenue that is, in the vast majority of cases, entirely theoretical or contingent on payments from unprofitable and unsustainable AI companies.
sentences.zh: 它们的崩溃是不可避免的，因为它们的持续付款是由客户收入支付的，而在绝大多数情况下，这些收入完全是理论上的，或者取决于来自无利可图和不可持续的人工智能公司的付款。

blocks.html_en: What differs this from the subprime mortgage crisis is that the systemic risks aren’t driven by derivatives or complex financials but by the sheer scale of costs to build an AI data center, a catastrophic misunderstanding of the AI industry itself and the dangerous lending standards of private credit. When every single debt deal is over $500 million and usually numbering in the billions, we don’t need a vast web of different contracts to create a systemic risk, just clusters of projects that either fail to keep up with their SPVs’ debt or bonds that go unpaid by destitute or defunct data center developers.

sentences.en: What differs this from the subprime mortgage crisis is that the systemic risks aren’t driven by derivatives or complex financials but by the sheer scale of costs to build an AI data center, a catastrophic misunderstanding of the AI industry itself and the dangerous lending standards of private credit.
sentences.zh: 这与次贷危机的不同之处在于，系统性风险不是由衍生品或复杂的金融市场驱动的，而是由建设人工智能数据中心的巨大成本、对人工智能行业本身的灾难性误解以及私人信贷的危险贷款标准驱动的。

sentences.en: When every single debt deal is over $500 million and usually numbering in the billions, we don’t need a vast web of different contracts to create a systemic risk, just clusters of projects that either fail to keep up with their SPVs’ debt or bonds that go unpaid by destitute or defunct data center developers.
sentences.zh: 当每笔债务交易超过 5 亿美元且通常达到数十亿美元时，我们不需要大量不同的合同来产生系统性风险，只需要一系列项目，这些项目要么无法跟上 SPV 的债务，要么贫困或倒闭的数据中心开发商未偿还债券。

blocks.html_en: Also, please remember that it didn’t take massive losses to begin the great financial crisis — just hard hits to a few load-bearing pillars of the industry. Lehman Brothers suffered two sequential quarters of losses ($2.8bn in Q2 CY2008 and $3.9bn in Q3 CY2028) before it entered liquidation. Those losses weren’t what killed it, but rather, what those losses did to the broader market — as well as the perception of Lehman with potential saviors.

sentences.en: Also, please remember that it didn’t take massive losses to begin the great financial crisis — just hard hits to a few load-bearing pillars of the industry.
sentences.zh: 另外，请记住，这场巨大的金融危机并没有造成巨大损失，只是对行业的一些承重支柱造成了沉重打击。

sentences.en: Lehman Brothers suffered two sequential quarters of losses ($2.8bn in Q2 CY2008 and $3.9bn in Q3 CY2028) before it entered liquidation.
sentences.zh: 雷曼兄弟在进入清算之前连续两个季度亏损（2008 年第二季度亏损 28 亿美元，2028 年第三季度亏损 39 亿美元）。

sentences.en: Those losses weren’t what killed it, but rather, what those losses did to the broader market — as well as the perception of Lehman with potential saviors.
sentences.zh: 这些损失并不是杀死它的原因，而是这些损失对更广泛的市场以及雷曼兄弟对潜在救星的看法的影响。

blocks.html_en: Similarly, Bear Sterns failed after two hedge funds under its umbrella collapsed. While the monetary losses from these funds weren’t insignificant, they were something that, if everything else was fine, Bear Sterns could recover from them. Sadly, they occurred at a time when the market was spooked, and Bear was spectacularly over-leveraged, meaning that marginal losses would have a disproportionate impact on its balance sheet.

sentences.en: Similarly, Bear Sterns failed after two hedge funds under its umbrella collapsed.
sentences.zh: 同样，贝尔斯登在其旗下的两家对冲基金倒闭后也失败了。

sentences.en: While the monetary losses from these funds weren’t insignificant, they were something that, if everything else was fine, Bear Sterns could recover from them.
sentences.zh: 虽然这些基金造成的金钱损失并非微不足道，但如果一切顺利，贝尔斯登可以从中挽回损失。

sentences.en: Sadly, they occurred at a time when the market was spooked, and Bear was spectacularly over-leveraged, meaning that marginal losses would have a disproportionate impact on its balance sheet.
sentences.zh: 可悲的是，它们发生在市场受到惊吓的时候，而贝尔斯登的杠杆率惊人地过高，这意味着边际损失将对其资产负债表产生不成比例的影响。

blocks.html_en: For AI, those “hard hits” to the “load bearing pillars of the industry”  means a large amount of capacity flooding the market at once — such as that caused by the failure of a major compute customer, most likely OpenAI — or the slow arrival of new capacity that can’t find revenue to pay for it. Perhaps we get both.

sentences.en: For AI, those “hard hits” to the “load bearing pillars of the industry” means a large amount of capacity flooding the market at once — such as that caused by the failure of a major compute customer, most likely OpenAI — or the slow arrival of new capacity that can’t find revenue to pay for it.
sentences.zh: 对于人工智能来说，对“行业承载支柱”的“沉重打击”意味着大量容量立即涌入市场——例如由于主要计算客户（最有可能是 OpenAI）的失败而造成的——或者新容量缓慢到来而无法找到收入来支付。

sentences.en: Perhaps we get both.
sentences.zh: 也许我们两者都得到。

blocks.html_en: While the data center debt market might be much smaller than the trillions of dollars of (at least theoretical) securities that broke the back of the financial markets (I estimate somewhere between $500 billion and $750 billion), the risk — the actual underlying financing — is spread across the entire financial system, with every major bank and financial institution and the vast majority of asset management firms having billions or tens of billions of dollars’ worth of debt tied up in an impossible situation.

sentences.en: While the data center debt market might be much smaller than the trillions of dollars of (at least theoretical) securities that broke the back of the financial markets (I estimate somewhere between $500 billion and $750 billion), the risk — the actual underlying financing — is spread across the entire financial system, with every major bank and financial institution and the vast majority of asset management firms having billions or tens of billions of dollars’ worth of debt tied up in an impossible situation.
sentences.zh: 虽然数据中心债务市场可能比冲击金融市场的数万亿美元（至少理论上）证券要小得多（我估计在 5000 亿到 7500 亿美元之间），但风险（实际的基础融资）却分散在整个金融体系中，每家主要银行和金融机构以及绝大多数资产管理公司都背负着数十亿或数百亿美元的债务，陷入了一种不可能的境地。

blocks.html_en: The scenario I’m talking about is one where the vast majority of AI data centers go unused, and because the vast majority of data centers are paid out of customer revenues, 80% or more of the funds invested in AI data center debt will be lost. None of this is written to be alarmist or hyperbolic, and represents a rational position when compared to the fact that we have over 15 times the amount of data center capacity than we need, and there is little compelling evidence that there’s more than a few billion dollars in total demand.

sentences.en: The scenario I’m talking about is one where the vast majority of AI data centers go unused, and because the vast majority of data centers are paid out of customer revenues, 80% or more of the funds invested in AI data center debt will be lost.
sentences.zh: 我所说的场景是绝大多数AI数据中心闲置的情况，并且由于绝大多数数据中心是由客户收入支付的，因此投资于AI数据中心债务的资金将损失80%或更多。

sentences.en: None of this is written to be alarmist or hyperbolic, and represents a rational position when compared to the fact that we have over 15 times the amount of data center capacity than we need, and there is little compelling evidence that there’s more than a few billion dollars in total demand.
sentences.zh: 所有这些都不是危言耸听或夸张的，与我们的数据中心容量超过我们需要的 15 倍以上这一事实相比，它代表了一个理性的立场，而且几乎没有令人信服的证据表明总需求超过数十亿美元。

blocks.html_en:
Sidenote: If anything, I consider it a radical and actively deranged position to believe that there is demand for all this compute, and find it distressing that nobody else is making any substantive attempt to measure the actual demand for AI compute, or have any kind of meaningful discussion of what “we overbuilt capacity” means. 

This all makes me feel a little crazy. I’ve looked! I think I’ve looked harder than anyone else has! I’ve tracked down as many sources as I can of the actual amounts that people are paying to rent AI GPUs. The demand isn’t even remotely there!

I even found

a bizarre mid-2025 presentation from the owner of the land that Stargate Abilene is built on top of that prices the 1.2GW of capacity at around $10 billion a year. I imagine nobody wants to talk about this because it suggests that, at scale, AI compute only sells for about $8.3 billion a gigawatt annually, which makes the whole “data centers cost $100 billion a gigawatt” mathematics a little hard to reconcile with.

I need everybody to realize that the overbuild has real consequences, and that there’s no post-bubble economy to replace this one, because it’ll be just as expensive to run these things in 2030 as it is today.

sentences.en: Sidenote: If anything, I consider it a radical and actively deranged position to believe that there is demand for all this compute, and find it distressing that nobody else is making any substantive attempt to measure the actual demand for AI compute, or have any kind of meaningful discussion of what “we overbuilt capacity” means.
sentences.zh: 旁注：如果说有什么不同的话，我认为相信所有这些计算都有需求，这是一种激进且疯狂的立场，并且令人苦恼的是，没有其他人做出任何实质性的尝试来衡量人工智能计算的实际需求，或者对“我们过度建设的能力”的含义进行任何有意义的讨论。

sentences.zh: 这一切都让我感觉有点疯狂。

sentences.zh: 我看过了！

sentences.en: I think I’ve looked harder than anyone else has!

sentences.zh: 我已经尽可能多地追踪人们租用 AI GPU 的实际金额。

sentences.en: The demand isn’t even remotely there!I even found a bizarre mid-2025 presentation from the owner of the land that Stargate Abilene is built on top of that prices the 1.2GW of capacity at around $10 billion a year.
sentences.zh: 需求根本就不存在！我什至发现了 2025 年中期土地所有者的一份奇怪的演示，称阿比林星际之门是在其上建造的，其价格为每年 100 亿美元左右的 1.2GW 容量。

sentences.en: I imagine nobody wants to talk about this because it suggests that, at scale, AI compute only sells for about $8.3 billion a gigawatt annually, which makes the whole “data centers cost $100 billion a gigawatt” mathematics a little hard to reconcile with.I need everybody to realize that the overbuild has real consequences, and that there’s no post-bubble economy to replace this one, because it’ll be just as expensive to run these things in 2030 as it is today.

blocks.html_en: This will mean that effectively every single financial institution in the world will have to write off or mark down hundreds of millions or billions of dollars’ worth of loans — and when they go to sell the underlying assets, they’ll be dumping aging hopper and Blackwell GPUs into a market saturated with them, meaning that the salvage price they get — assuming they get one at all — will be negligible.

sentences.en: This will mean that effectively every single financial institution in the world will have to write off or mark down hundreds of millions or billions of dollars’ worth of loans — and when they go to sell the underlying assets, they’ll be dumping aging hopper and Blackwell GPUs into a market saturated with them, meaning that the salvage price they get — assuming they get one at all — will be negligible.
sentences.zh: 这意味着世界上每家金融机构都必须注销或减记价值数亿或数十亿美元的贷款，而当他们出售基础资产时，他们会将老化的 hopper 和 Blackwell GPU 倾销到已经饱和的市场中，这意味着他们获得的残值（假设他们真的获得了残值）可以忽略不计。

blocks.html_en: This is the data center equivalent of subprime loans defaulting, except instead of hundreds of thousands of loans being the trigger, all it takes is ten or fifteen of them to send the industry into a panic.

sentences.en: This is the data center equivalent of subprime loans defaulting, except instead of hundreds of thousands of loans being the trigger, all it takes is ten or fifteen of them to send the industry into a panic.
sentences.zh: 这在数据中心相当于次级贷款违约，只不过不是数十万笔贷款成为触发因素，只需要十到十五笔贷款就能让整个行业陷入恐慌。

blocks.html_en: It’s easy to dismiss this entirely as “rich people problems,” but AI data centers are increasingly funded — both directly and through private credit — using pension and insurance funds that rely on these (theoretical) payments for future yield to pay out premiums.

sentences.en: It’s easy to dismiss this entirely as “rich people problems,” but AI data centers are increasingly funded — both directly and through private credit — using pension and insurance funds that rely on these (theoretical) payments for future yield to pay out premiums.
sentences.zh: 人们很容易将其完全视为“富人问题”，但人工智能数据中心越来越多地使用养老金和保险基金（直接或通过私人信贷）获得资金，这些基金依赖于这些（理论上的）未来收益支付来支付保费。

blocks.html_en: I’ll give you some examples.

sentences.en: I’ll give you some examples.
sentences.zh: 我给你举一些例子。

blocks.html_en: • Australian AI infrastructure company Morrison, backers of CDC (the largest data center operator in the country), recently convinced Japanese bank SMBC to allow it to invest its pension funds in AI data centers in the country.

sentences.en: Australian AI infrastructure company Morrison, backers of CDC (the largest data center operator in the country), recently convinced Japanese bank SMBC to allow it to invest its pension funds in AI data centers in the country.
sentences.zh: 澳大利亚人工智能基础设施公司莫里森是 CDC（该国最大的数据中心运营商）的支持者，最近说服日本银行 SMBC 允许其将其养老基金投资于该国的人工智能数据中心。

blocks.html_en: • IPI Partners, a one of the largest private data center investment firms that is now owned by Blue Owl, has a limited partner (read: people funding it) base, per Deutsche Bank, split into equal 25% chunks made up of sovereign wealth funds, family offices, public pensions, and insurance/private pension endowments.

sentences.en: IPI Partners, a one of the largest private data center investment firms that is now owned by Blue Owl, has a limited partner (read: people funding it) base, per Deutsche Bank, split into equal 25% chunks made up of sovereign wealth funds, family offices, public pensions, and insurance/private pension endowments.
sentences.zh: IPI Partners 是最大的私人数据中心投资公司之一，目前由 Blue Owl 拥有，根据德意志银行的说法，它拥有一个有限合伙人（即为其提供资金的人）基础，分为由主权财富基金、家族办公室、公共养老金和保险/私人养老金捐赠基金组成的 25% 的等额部分。

blocks.html_en:
• The California State Teacher’s Retirement System is the biggest investor in Blue Owl’s publicly-traded Blue Owl Capital Corporation fund.

sentences.en: In 2024, Blue Owl acquired insurer Kuvare, which won the Great Des Moines Partnership’s “Deal Of The Decade” Award in 2023 for funding Meta’s Altoona-based data center, which was, at the time, Meta’s largest data center.

blocks.html_en: • CDPQ, one of Quebec’s largest pension funds, invested in CoreWeave’s $7.5 billion DDTL 1.0, as part of its CDPQ American Fixed Income V Inc fund.

blocks.html_en:
• A few weeks ago, Asset manager Apollo Global raised $35 billion for Broadcom to build Google TPUs for Anthropic to lease, and did so funded by billions of dollars of insurance annuities it’s able to play with as a result of its acquisition/merger with insurance and retirement firm Athene. If these payments aren’t made (though Broadcom has backstopped them), it will directly hit Athene’s ability to pay out insurance and retirement premiums.
    • One worrying quote from the piece, emphasis mine: “What also sets Apollo apart is its homegrown trading operation, further blurring the lines between the alternative asset manager and Wall Street banks. It has also become one of the largest forces in insurance, prompting concerns that the firm and its peers are ramping up risk in a once-sleepy part of finance, and at a pace that makes it difficult for regulators to keep up.”

sentences.en: A few weeks ago, Asset manager Apollo Global raised $35 billion for Broadcom to build Google TPUs for Anthropic to lease, and did so funded by billions of dollars of insurance annuities it’s able to play with as a result of its acquisition/merger with insurance and retirement firm Athene.
sentences.zh: 几周前，资产管理公司 Apollo Global 为 Broadcom 筹集了 350 亿美元，用于建造可供 Anthropic 租赁的 Google TPU，这一资金来源是通过收购/合并保险和退休公司 Athene 获得的数十亿美元的保险年金。

sentences.zh: 如果不支付这些款项（尽管博通已经提供了支持），这将直接影响 Athene 支付保险和退休金的能力。这篇文章中引用了一段令人担忧的言论，我强调的是：“阿波罗与众不同的另一个原因是其本土交易业务，进一步模糊了另类资产管理公司和华尔街银行之间的界限。

blocks.html_en: The other problem with the “private” part of private credit is that we don’t really know how much data center exposure pension and insurance funds and the insurance/retirement funds of asset managers actually have. What we do know is that private credit is sinking hundreds of billions of dollars of people’s retirements and insurance premiums into deals based on obfuscated valuations and questionable underwriting standards.

sentences.en: The other problem with the “private” part of private credit is that we don’t really know how much data center exposure pension and insurance funds and the insurance/retirement funds of asset managers actually have.
sentences.zh: 私人信贷“私人”部分的另一个问题是，我们并不真正知道数据中心的养老金和保险基金以及资产管理公司的保险/退休基金实际有多少风险敞口。

sentences.en: What we do know is that private credit is sinking hundreds of billions of dollars of people’s retirements and insurance premiums into deals based on obfuscated valuations and questionable underwriting standards.
sentences.zh: 我们所知道的是，私人信贷正在将数千亿美元的人们的退休金和保险费投入到基于模糊估值和可疑承保标准的交易中。
